name: task-ESE-0020
title: GPU 3D physics optimizations: research and implement state-of-the-art improvements
description:
Research and implement state-of-the-art GPU physics optimizations. Current 3D demo bottlenecks:

1. **Per-frame readback stall**: We mapAsync every frame to read positions back to CPU for rendering. This serializes GPU and CPU work. Fix: render directly from GPU storage buffers (zero-copy rendering) using WebGPU render pipeline that reads the same storage buffers the compute shader writes to. Eliminate readback entirely for rendering.

2. **No double buffering**: We read and write the same position buffers each frame. Fix: ping-pong pattern — compute writes to buffer A while render reads buffer B, then swap. Eliminates read-after-write hazards.

3. **Indirect dispatch**: We hardcode workgroup counts on CPU. Fix: use dispatchWorkgroupsIndirect() with a GPU-written indirect buffer, especially useful when body count varies.

4. **Workgroup occupancy**: Current workgroup size 64 is correct for most GPUs but we do no experimentation. Profile different sizes.

5. **Grid memory**: 64^3 * 4 * 4 = 4MB grid cleared every frame. Consider smaller grid with wrapping hash, or only clearing populated cells.

References:
- https://webgpufundamentals.org/webgpu/lessons/webgpu-optimization.html
- https://toji.dev/webgpu-best-practices/indirect-draws.html
- https://markaicode.com/webgpu-physics-simulation-1m-particles/
acceptanceCriteria:
- Zero-copy rendering: compute storage buffers used directly by render pipeline (no mapAsync for rendering)
- Double-buffered position data (ping-pong)
- Documented performance improvement vs current baseline
- 3D bouncing spheres demo updated with optimizations
- Demo growth rate doubled (10% per second instead of 5%)
demoDeliverable: Storybook demo: 3D bouncing spheres with optimized pipeline, doubled growth rate, showing higher body count at 30fps than the unoptimized version.
testingScenarios:
- No mapAsync calls in the render loop (verified by code review)
- FPS improvement at same body count vs baseline
- Higher body count at 30fps threshold vs baseline
stakeholderUnderstanding: The main bottleneck is per-frame readback (mapAsync). Eliminating this by rendering directly from GPU storage buffers is the biggest win. Double-buffering and warmup are secondary.
